exercises.title: Variables & Arithmetic
exercises.description: Create variables x = 15 and y = 4. Print their sum, difference, and product on separate lines.
exercises.starterCode: # Declare x and y, print sum, difference, product
exercises.hint: Use print(x + y), print(x - y), print(x * y).
exercises.solutionCode:
x = 15
y = 4
print(x + y)
print(x - y)
print(x * y)

exercises.title: String Formatting
exercises.description: Create a variable name = "Python". Print I love Python! using an f-string.
exercises.starterCode: # Use an f-string to print the message

exercises.title: List Operations
exercises.description: Create a list fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]. Add "date" to the list and print the full list.
exercises.starterCode: # Create list, add an item, and print
exercises.hint: Use the .append() method to add items to a list.

exercises.title: For Loop & Range
exercises.description: Use a for loop with range() to print numbers from 1 to 5.
exercises.starterCode: # Print 1 to 5 using a for loop
exercises.hint: Use for i in range(1, 6): print(i)

exercises.title: Function — Factorial
exercises.description: Write a function factorial(n) that returns the factorial of n. Print factorial(5).
exercises.starterCode: # Define factorial function and test it
exercises.hint: Use a loop or recursion: if n <= 1 return 1, else return n * factorial(n-1).

exercises.title: Dictionary
exercises.description: Create a dictionary student = {"name": "Alice", "grade": "A", "age": 20}. Print each key-value pair on a separate line in the format key: value.
exercises.starterCode: # Create dictionary and print key-value pairs
exercises.hint: Use a for loop: for key, value in student.items(): print(f"{key}: {value}")

exercises.title: List Comprehension
exercises.description: Use a list comprehension to create a list of squares from 1 to 10. Print the result.
exercises.solutionCode:
squares = [x**2 for x in range(1, 11)]
print(squares)

exercises.title: Class & Object
exercises.description: Create a class Dog with __init__ taking name and breed. Add a method bark() that returns "Woof!". Create an instance and print its name and bark.

exercises.title: File-like Operations & Error Handling
exercises.description: Write a function safe_divide(a, b) that returns a / b but catches ZeroDivisionError and returns "Cannot divide by zero". Test with safe_divide(10, 0) and safe_divide(10, 2).
exercises.hint: Use try/except ZeroDivisionError inside the function.
